summary: McKenzie recounts an unexpected brush with internet fame after commenting on Matt Cutts' blog during a lunch break, only to find himself on the front page of WordPress by evening. He quickly pivots back to his regular business concerns, noting issues with his AdWords conversion tracking showing 0% and Google Analytics reporting zero traffic for three days, which he attributes to system glitches rather than actual problems.
key_points: A casual blog comment about Google legal papers unexpectedly went viral, landing him on the WordPress front page
He deliberately returns focus to his core business of selling software to teachers rather than chasing internet attention
His AdWords and Analytics data appear corrupted, showing impossible 0% conversion rates and zero traffic
He applies statistical reasoning to recognize the data must be a system error rather than real performance decline
money_quotes: I finished my workday, went out to bid fairwell to a friend leaving the country, and when I get back to my computer I'm on the front page of WordPress.
Google certainly inspires some passion in people, what can I say.
Anyhow, back to comfortable anonymity and the pressing business of saving teachers time and money.
I'm showing a conversion rate of 0%, which is highly statistically improbable.
Ahh well, kinks in the system, I'm reasonably sure they'll get cleared up soon.
key_insight: Unexpected internet attention is a distraction; the real work is getting back to building your business and monitoring your metrics.
